Father Adams, the astronomer a,
Gonzaga college. says that after
May 20 Halley's comet will be Wis
ible in the evening in the eastern
horizon.


Charles Reign Scovilie, the evan
gelist conducting a revival at the
Central Christian church, under
the auspices of the churches of that
faith in the city, last night held
his second meeting and delivered a
sermon on prayer.


The Spokane County conques,
campaign and convention was
, launched this afternoon at the
Vincent NJ. E. church, and will con
tinue until Saturday. There are
zoo Sunday schools in the union
which will be represented by soc
delegates.


n. T. Robbins has sold the south
west corner of First and Maple
having 100 feet of frontage on First
by 1O7 on Maple, to Richard j
Howard for $1S,5O0.


Following trip by the mayor
and Contractor Huetter to the Ken
wood standpipe, it Is announced
that Huetter will now proceed tc
finish the job. The standpipe has
yet to receive it's waterproofins
and connecting mains must be laid
There has been nothing done to
ward completing the work in the
last few decks.


The official count Of the vote on
the park bond election will be made
Tuesday night by the city council
The tally sheets have been return
ed to the office of the city clerk
who says that the unofficial returns
show no variation from the figures
first given out, and that the bond
issue has carried by a majority OF
Is. A change of few votes in the
| official count might have a serious
result as to the outcome of the
election.


Pardons have arrived from Gov
ernor Hay for Hartwell Shippey
Otto Justh and Thomas Whitehead
the last of the alleged !. w. W.
"conspirators," sentenced to the
county Jail as the result of the
street speaking campaign last No.
vember. The release of the men is
part of the compact entered into by
the prosecutors office, the police
and the l. w. w.


Walter n. Emerson has brought
suit for $1509 damages against the
Thompson Starratt Construction
company, erecting the Old National
bank building, for personal injuries
received in January while employ.
ed in making the excavation for the
building.


In order that none bay be over.
looked, the census bureau, after
checking up its first figures, will
see that any omitted names are
added to the rolls.


The funeral Of Henrietta Hill
who died at S617 Division street
May 2, was held from the Smith un.
dertaking establishment at 2:3O
today. The Rev. Paul H. eros.
chupf officiated and burial was at
Greenwood cemetery.


Contractor John Huetter has
again been absolved by the board
of public works from any RESPONSE
